reasoning_model_1: The model generates the audio content 'Good morning! The weather is really nice today, I feel great.' with perfect accuracy and fluency. Regarding the core paralinguistic feature 'Pitch', the model successfully adopts a consistently high fundamental frequency (F0) throughout the entire clip (0:00-0:05). The voice timbre shifts towards a younger or more stylized persona to accommodate this high pitch naturally, maintaining the elevation without sounding mechanically pitch-shifted. The result is a distinct and unmistakable high-pitched delivery that adheres strictly to the user's command.
reasoning_model_2: The model accurately reproduces the text 'Good morning! The weather is really nice today, I feel great.' with a very natural and fluent delivery. model_comparison: Both models produce high-quality, fluent audio that matches the text script perfectly. When comparing the specific adherence to the 'High Pitch' constraint, Model 1 (T1) demonstrates a significantly clearer and more consistent application of the feature. T1 maintains a noticeably higher fundamental frequency throughout the utterance, effectively distinguishing the output as 'high pitched'. Model 2 (T2) delivers a very natural and pleasant enthusiastic tone, but its pitch, while bright, falls within a more standard speaking range and does not exhibit the same degree of pitch elevation as T1.
